data.title: Newegg Commerce partners with PayPal for AI-powered shopping
data.description:
Newegg Commerce (NEGG) will integrate PayPal's (PYPL) agentic commerce services to enable AI-powered shopping on portals, including Perplexity.

Through the partnership with PayPal (PYPL), merchants like Newegg (NEGG) can become discoverable, and consumers can buy from merchants directly with Perplexity.

Newegg (NEGG) retains the merchant of record status to preserve customer relationships and brand visibility throughout the AI shopping experience.

“AI is redefining the customer shopping journey, and over the past two years we’ve leveraged AI to build entirely new experiences for our customers,” said Newegg CEO Anthony Chow.

data.title: TOMI Environmental Solutions announces $3M custom integration pipeline
data.description:
• TOMI Environmental Solutions (TOMZ) announces progress in its custom integration pipeline, currently valued at approximately $3M, signaling significant momentum in the life sciences sector. 
  • The company reports ten active projects across its SteraMist Integrated System and Custom Engineered System platforms, serving both new and existing customers.
  • Shares of TOMI Environmental Solutions (TOMZ) were up more than 8% in premarket trading.

data.title: Kyndryl in focus as Guggenheim starts with Buy rating
data.description:
Kyndryl (KD) was in focus on Wednesday as Guggenheim started coverage on the tech company with a Buy rating and $30 price target.

Shares rose fractionally in premarket trading. 

“Since its 2021 spin-off from IBM, Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. (KD) management has been actively reshaping its business through the 'Triple A' program—Alliances, Advanced Delivery, and Accounts—to elevate quality of signings, automate and standardize delivery, and enforce contract discipline, driving improved profitability and positive free cash flow in a large, slow-growing TAM segment with high barriers to entry and a limited number of scaled competitors,” analyst Jonathan Lee wrote in a note to clients. “Though recent deal slippage has hindered revenue performance, progress on profitability is encouraging, particularly in a challenging macroeconomic environment. As such, we believe KD is among the best positioned in the infrastructure outsourcing segment to pivot to stability over the medium-term, underpinning our initiation of coverage with a Buy rating and $30 price target.” 

Delving deeper, Lee said the 2024 investor day, in which company management laid out its medium-term ambitions, was helpful. By highlighting its goal to triple cash flow, double pretax earnings and generate single-digit revenue growth by fiscal 2028, the company is focusing on “building a 'healthier' services business by prioritizing portfolio quality,” Lee explained.

“While we remain in [the] early innings of the company's turnaround, we see positive traction across the company's 'Triple A' initiative, which help underpin our Buy rating as post-spin deal signings contribute to greater profitability in the near-to-medium-term,” Lee added.

data.title: Scope Technologies announces name change and corporate rebrand to QSE
data.description:
• Scope Technologies (OTCQB:SCPCF) said on Wednesday that it is undergoing a corporate rebrand to QSE - Quantum Secure Encryption Corp.
  • The rebrand represents a strategic evolution for the company as it continues its mission to advance global data protection through next-generation quantum security and encryption technologies.
  • The company will begin transitioning all corporate materials, communications, and branding to QSE in the coming weeks. The company anticipates its new ticker symbols for the CSE will be "QSE".

data.title: Deere's weak 2026 guidance points to prolonged agricultural downturn
data.description:
Deere (DE) -4.8% pre-market Wednesday after beating estimates for FQ4 earnings and revenues, but forecasting FY 2026 earnings well below expectations, pressured by tariff impacts and weaker margins from its large tractors.

Q4 net income fell to $1.065 billion, or $3.93/share, from $1.245 billion, or $4.55/share, in the year-earlier quarter, citing elevated costs linked in part to tariffs, but revenues rose 11% Y/Y to $12.39 billion, due to higher shipments and prices.

In August, Deere (DE) warned it expected a pre-tax tariff impact of nearly $600 million in FY 2025.

Q4 by segment: Production & Precision Agriculture net sales up 10% Y/Y to $4.74 billion, but operating profit down 8% to $604 million and operating margin 12.7% vs. 15.3% a year ago; Small Agriculture & Turf net sales up 6.5% Y/Y to $2.46 billion, but operating profit down 89% to $25 million and operating margin 1% vs. 10.1% a year ago; and Construction & Forestry net sales up 27% Y/Y to $3.38 billion, with operating profit up 6% to $348 million and operating margin 10.3% vs. 12.3% a year ago.

For FY 2026, Deere (DE) guided for net income in the $4.0 billion-$4.75 billion, below analyst consensus estimate of $5.33 billion, while expecting a 15%-20% decline in sales for its large agriculture in the U.S. and Canada, while seeing flat results in South America for tractors and crop-cutting combines.

"We believe 2026 will mark the bottom of the large ag cycle," May said. "While ongoing margin pressures from tariffs and persistent challenges in the large ag sector remain, our commitment to inventory management and cost control, coupled with expected growth in Small Agriculture & Turf and Construction & Forestry, positions us to effectively manage the business and seize emerging opportunities as market conditions begin to recover."

data.title: Fannie Mae reports 0.9% rise in October guaranty book of business
data.description:
Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) said on Wednesday that its guaranty book of business increased at a compound annualized rate of 0.9% in October.

October conventional single-family serious delinquency rate remained flat at 0.54%. Multifamily serious delinquency rate increased 3 basis points to 0.71%.

As of October 31, Fannie Mae's maximum exposure to Freddie Mac collateral that was included in outstanding Fannie Mae resecuritizations stood at $186.5B.

data.title: SuperX announces $20 million buyback
data.description:
• SuperX AI Technology (SUPX) authorized a share repurchase program under which the company may repurchase up to $20 million shares over the next one year.
  • The company currently has a market cap of ~$691 million.
  • The stock price traded 2.6% higher on Wednesday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Stock index futures rise as investors assess economic data
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Wednesday, as discussions on rate cuts were in focus while investors assessed some macroeconomic data. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.4% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

Initial jobless claims surprised lower in the week ended November 22. Durable goods orders slowed in September, as expected.

"In terms of those various themes, the Fed remained in focus, as the latest batch of U.S. data kept the path open to a December rate cut," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said. 

On Tuesday, core producer prices rose less than expected in September, while the headline PPI came in line. Retail sales were also less than expected in September.

data.description:

  • Payable Dec. 23; for shareholders of record Dec. 9; ex-div Dec. 9.
  • See CGY:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Ambarella falls despite 'solid' Q3 results drawing praise from analysts
data.description:
Ambarella's (AMBA) stock fell about 8% premarket on Wednesday despite third quarter results beating estimates, which largely drew positive reactions from analysts.

Morgan Stanley maintained its Overweight rating on the shares with a $96 price target noting that Internet of Things, or IoT, execution delivered another solid quarter.

"Edge-AI momentum remains strong, with IoT driving near-term upside and solid operating leverage despite slight mix-driven margin pressure. We remain positive on their long-term auto prospects and continue to have conviction in their technology, but IoT will carry growth for now," said analysts led by Joseph Moore.

The analysts noted that as expected, IoT drove the bulk of strength this quarter, led by the portable video and other segment, where multiple categories — including action camera, wearables, and panoramic systems — continue to show strong momentum.

The analysts added that gross margins came in below expectations in the guidance, as the fourth quarter will carry a larger mix of high-volume customers. This lowers the percentage margin, but Ambarella stressed that their priority is growing gross profit dollars. Management is willing to consider taking lower margins in specific volume opportunities, but they are still committed to their 59-62% target range on the corporate level, the analysts noted.

While the company expects mix headwinds to persist, higher revenue and steady operating expenditure are creating solid operating leverage, according to the analysts. The analysts expect fourth quarter operating profit to increase even as gross margin declines. 

"Looking ahead, Ambarella expressed confidence that the IoT product cycle remains strong, with demand broadening beyond security into portable video, drones, and early robotics. We expect these edge-AI categories to keep expanding and driving steady IoT growth into next year, where we expect IoT to outpace automotive growth year-over-year," said Moore and his team.

Needham kept its Buy rating on Ambarella's shares with a $100 price target.

"Ambarella delivered another beat and raise driven by increasing demand in edge AI applications. Our key takeaways include: 1) The stronger F4Q26 revenue guide has enabled the company to raise its FY26 revenue outlook to +36-38% Y/Y (vs. a prior forecast of +31-35% Y/Y); 2) F4Q26 NG gross margin is coming in lower than expected due to mix shift towards higher volume customers. Despite the lower GM, our revenue, operating income, and EPS estimates all move higher in F4Q26," said analysts led by Quinn Bolton.

Other key takeaways, according to the analysts — the company's management highlighted three beneficial factors as edge AI applications continue to expand; and that in the third quarter of fiscal 2026, the company received more than a dozen design wins across Automotive and IoT applications.
